mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 6.5 percent, labor market slack dissipates rapidly, triggering wage growth expectations that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.5 percent within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with accelerating nonfarm payrolls indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies aggregate demand and sustains positive equity momentum over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative rank correlation with forward returns in more than 50 percent of out-of-sample months, indicating decoupled housing and labor dynamics.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing demand signals a supply glut that compresses corporate margins and equity valuations, producing negative forward returns specifically when the Federal Reserve is actively raising policy rates.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO growth decelerates below 0.5 percent annualized during periods of rising policy rates.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized confirms robust economic expansion, which historically precedes earnings upgrades and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate exceeds 2.5 percent annualized
failure_condition: The signal yields a negative predictive rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDP growth exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in core inflation and the federal funds rate indicate aggressive monetary tightening to combat price pressures, which increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when both CPI and FEDFUNDS rise concurrently, demonstrating that inflation and rate hikes do not jointly suppress valuations.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic resilience, which supports corporate revenue trajectories and generates positive equity returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows a negative rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during months of payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in consumer credit outstanding signals tightening financial conditions and reduced household spending capacity, which mechanically depresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines more than 5 percent over a 3-month window
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of instances following a 3-month RSAFS decline exceeding 5 percent, invalidating the credit-spending channel.
